# Graph Report - louisville  (2026-07-05)

## Corpus Check
- 8972 files · ~8,625,362 words
- Verdict: corpus is large enough that graph structure adds value.

## Summary
- 64890 nodes · 114389 edges · 4885 communities (3657 shown, 1228 thin omitted)
- Extraction: 91% EXTRACTED · 9% INFERRED · 0% AMBIGUOUS · INFERRED: 10840 edges (avg confidence: 0.62)
- Token cost: 0 input · 0 output

## Graph Freshness
- Built from commit: `931bc5af`
- Run `git rev-parse HEAD` and compare to check if the graph is stale.
- Run `graphify update .` after code changes (no API cost).

Cohesion: 0.08
Nodes (24): <human_verification>, must_have 1: After a fresh install, calling globalThis.fsbInstallIdentity.getOrCreateInstallUuid() mints a v4 UUID and persists it to chrome.storage.local under fsbInstallUuid (IDENT-01), must_have 2: Calling getOrCreateInstallUuid() a second time returns the SAME UUID -- no re-mint -- across both onInstalled and onStartup lifecycles (IDENT-02), must_have 3: When chrome.storage.local is unavailable / throws, getOrCreateInstallUuid() resolves to null with NO throw and NO session-only UUID fallback (IDENT-03), must_have 4: Module never reads or writes chrome.storage.sync; storage namespace is exclusively chrome.storage.local (IDENT-04), must_have 5: Module export surface is identity-only -- getOrCreateInstallUuid, isTelemetryOptedOut, setTelemetryOptOut, key constants -- and contains no URL / prompt / DOM / clipboard fields (IDENT-05), must_have 6: The Control Panel Advanced Settings tab shows a 'Privacy & Telemetry' card with a 'Send anonymous usage data' toggle that renders ON by default on a fresh profile (CONS-01), must_have 7: Clicking the toggle writes fsbTelemetryOptOut = !current to chrome.storage.local synchronously and the visible toggle state reflects the new value within 100ms (CONS-02) (+16 more)

### Community 2202 - "Info"
Cohesion: 0.13
Nodes (14): IN-01: `_trim` helper is module-private but `computeUrl` / `computeHeaders` re-trim apiKey twice in the autopilot path (once at compute, once when constructing factory args), IN-02: `var` in modern code -- `agent-loop.js:1049-1050` uses `var _cfg` / `var _settings` for new code while the surrounding file uses `let`/`const`, IN-03: Magic string fallback `'http://localhost:1234'` duplicated between options.js / agent-loop.js / lattice-host.js without a shared constant, IN-04: `chrome.runtime.sendMessage` from offscreen back to SW (line 314, 325) drops errors silently with `.catch((err) => console.warn(...))` -- if the SW is in the process of being evicted, the receipt is lost without a retry queue, IN-05: Smoke test's `loadOffscreenHandlerSource` uses `_offscreenModulePromise` module-level cache -- if a future Part 7 needs a fresh load of the offscreen module, the cache silently returns the cross-mock module, IN-06: `lattice-host.js:483-486` constructs an error with `.providerError = text` where `text` may be the entire HTTP body -- could include masked API key echoes (xAI `'xa***cy'` style); the bridge then forwards it to the agent-loop catch which logs it via `automationLogger.error`, Info, Phase 6: Code Review Report (+6 more)

Cohesion: 0.18

### Community 2739 - "Critical Pitfalls"
Cohesion: 0.22
Nodes (9): Critical Pitfalls, Pitfall 2: Cloning the imperative model — the bundled head grows into per-app handler sprawl and trips MV3 "code fetched as data" / Web-Store ban at scale, Pitfall 3: One-size codegen vs. per-app auth diversity → descriptors that bind to the wrong shape and silently return wrong/empty results, Pitfall 4: Descriptor-vs-recipe side-effect mismatch — a mis-authored side-effect class under-states a destructive op, at 2,523-op scale, Pitfall 5: Token/secret leakage in logs and the audit trail, multiplied across 119 apps' bespoke auth material, Pitfall 6: Catalog/search performance and SW startup degradation at thousands of descriptors, Pitfall 7: Recipe rot across 119 apps overwhelms self-heal — the designed steady state becomes a thundering herd, Pitfall 8: ToS / legal exposure — importing ToS-hostile app categories as invocable API capabilities (+1 more)

### Community 3244 - "Grounding: the 5 real auth patterns observed in OpenTabs source"
Cohesion: 0.33
Nodes (6): Grounding: the 5 real auth patterns observed in OpenTabs source, Pattern A — Same-origin cookie, GET, no token (pure T1b recipe), Pattern B — Same-origin cookie + scraped CSRF/anti-forgery token (T1a handler), Pattern C — Split-token / webpack-extracted / WebSocket-captured token (T1a handler, hard), Pattern D — SEPARATE API origin + bearer from localStorage (T1a *only if* CORS permits; else FSB-FORBIDDEN → T2/T3), Pattern E — Host-SDK trampoline (`gapi.client.request`) (T1a handler, Google-specific)
